medications.indication_rationale: Sublingual GTN aborts and prophylactically prevents anginal episodes by reducing preload and dilating coronary vessels. Used at symptom onset, or prophylactically 5 minutes before the known trigger (walking up the hill). It is also the safety-net tool: failure to respond to GTN flags possible ACS.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1 spray (400 microgram) sublingually at symptom onset or 5 minutes before predictable exertion; may repeat every 5 minutes to a maximum of 3 doses — if pain persists beyond 15 minutes / after 3 doses, call 000
contraindications_in_this_case: Concurrent phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitor use (e.g. sildenafil) — risk of severe hypotension; confirm before prescribing
interactions_flagged_for_this_patient: Additive hypotension with the patient's existing antihypertensive — advise to sit down when using GTN

medications.indication_rationale: Low-dose aspirin reduces atherothrombotic events in suspected coronary artery disease and is standard secondary prevention for angina, pending confirmation and unless contraindicated.
contraindications_in_this_case: Active peptic ulcer disease or aspirin allergy — neither reported here; confirm before commencing

medications.indication_rationale: The patient is already on a statin for hypercholesterolaemia; continue and optimise for secondary prevention of coronary disease. Dose not specified in the source — to be set by the assessing clinician.
Blood-pressure control lowers myocardial oxygen demand and cardiovascular risk; continue current therapy. Agent/dose not specified in the source.

medications.drug_name: Empirical proton pump inhibitor as the primary treatment (attributing pain to reflux)
medications.indication_rationale: Attributing new exertional, left-arm-radiating chest pain to reflux and treating with a PPI instead of arranging urgent cardiac assessment is a dangerous error of commission that risks missing coronary disease/ACS.

medications.drug_name: Opioid or NSAID analgesia for 'musculoskeletal' chest pain
medications.indication_rationale: The pain is not musculoskeletal; analgesia would mask cardiac symptoms and NSAIDs raise cardiovascular risk. Not appropriate as management of this presentation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Intentionally stop and rest BEFORE reaching the known pain threshold on the hill, and use GTN spray prophylactically 5 minutes before the climb.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Pre-empting the oxygen supply/demand mismatch prevents the ischaemic episode and restores confidence and mobility.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: If the patient smokes, offer smoking-cessation support (nicotine replacement/referral).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Smoking is a major modifiable coronary risk factor; status not recorded in the source — assess and address.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Maintain regular activity within the symptom threshold once ACS is excluded and the pattern is confirmed stable; graded activity guided by the assessing clinician.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Deconditioning worsens angina; structured activity within tolerance is protective once safety is established.
Supports secondary prevention of coronary artery disease.

patient_education_points.point: This kind of new chest pain that comes on with effort and travels to the arm needs to be checked in person urgently with a heart tracing (ECG) — it should not be diagnosed or dismissed over a phone or chat consult.

The likely cause is a stable narrowing of the heart's arteries: when you exert yourself the heart needs more blood than the narrowed vessel can deliver, causing the heavy ache; resting lowers the demand and the pain eases.
Carry the GTN spray at all times and use it at the first heavy sensation or before the hill; sit down when you use it. Start the daily aspirin unless told otherwise.
Do NOT push through the pain — stop and rest immediately.
Formal testing (a stress test or CT scan of the heart arteries) is needed to see how narrow the arteries are and whether stents or bypass surgery might be required.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment now; then GP review within 48-72 hours to review the baseline ECG and expedite cardiology diagnostics
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Result of the same-day ECG (and troponin if done), symptom pattern and any interval rest/nocturnal pain, blood pressure and lipid control, and progress of the fast-track stress test / CT coronary angiography referral
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any chest pain at rest, pain waking from sleep, pain lasting >15 minutes or not relieved by 3 GTN doses, or new breathlessness/collapse — call 000 immediately rather than waiting for review

must_include_items: Recognise the history as cardiac-type (anginal) chest pain and keep acute coronary syndrome on the differential
State that a new cardiac-type chest pain cannot be diagnosed or excluded via telehealth and arrange URGENT same-day in-person assessment with a 12-lead ECG
Provide explicit safety-netting to call 000 for pain at rest, pain waking from sleep, or pain lasting >15 minutes / not relieved after 3 GTN doses
Arrange fast-track cardiology investigation (exercise stress test or CT coronary angiography)
Advise not to push through the pain — stop and rest immediately
acceptable_alternatives: Directing the patient to an emergency department (T4) instead of urgent same-day GP/urgent care if in-person urgent primary care is not accessible — over-triage here is acceptable and safe
Recommending aspirin 100 mg and short-acting GTN as first-line pharmacotherapy pending assessment
errors_of_omission: Failing to arrange any urgent in-person assessment / ECG
Failing to safety-net the rest-pain / prolonged-pain / GTN-unresponsive triggers
Failing to keep ACS on the differential for new-onset chest pain
errors_of_commission: Remotely 'diagnosing' stable angina and reassuring the patient without objective testing
Attributing the pain to reflux or musculoskeletal causes and treating with a PPI/analgesia instead of cardiac assessment
Under-triaging to a routine (non-urgent) GP appointment or self-care
Inventing or advising a specific GTN/aspirin dose beyond the source without clinician oversight, or advising the patient to drive themselves during an acute episode
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES recognising cardiac-type chest pain, arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment with an ECG (i.e. not diagnosing/clearing remotely), AND giving the 000 safety-netting triggers. Remote reassurance without objective assessment is an automatic fail.
